title: hierochloe alpina subsp. orthantha
intro: Hierochloe alpina subsp. orthantha, also known as sweet gale, is a perennial plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an expectorant, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Hierochloe alpina subsp. orthantha include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and cardiovascular issues.
